title: Smart Activation Modal Button — Block 46

requirements.text: In ActivateSportScreen, import useSport from SportContext and destructure sportStatus, currentSport, and deactivateSport. Derive a boolean isActive = sportStatus === 'active'.
acceptanceCriteria: ActivateSportScreen imports useSport from SportContext without errors
sportStatus, currentSport, and deactivateSport are correctly destructured from useSport()
isActive evaluates to true when sportStatus is 'active' and false otherwise

requirements.text: In ActivateSportScreen, replace the static activation button label with a dynamic label: when isActive is true display 'Deactivate {currentSport}' (e.g. 'Deactivate Soccer'), otherwise display 'Activate My Sport'.
acceptanceCriteria: Button label reads 'Activate My Sport' when no sport is active
Button label reads 'Deactivate {Sport}' (e.g. 'Deactivate Soccer') when a sport is active
Label updates reactively when sport status changes without a page reload

requirements.text: In ActivateSportScreen, update the button's onClick handler so that when isActive is true it calls deactivateSport(), and when isActive is false it calls the existing handleActivate() function.
acceptanceCriteria: Tapping the button when sport is active calls deactivateSport() and transitions sportStatus away from 'active'
Tapping the button when sport is inactive calls the existing handleActivate() function
No runtime crashes occur during either action
Map and Events pages continue to auto-filter by the active sport after activation
After deactivation, Map and Events pages revert to unfiltered or default state

constraints: Do not modify SportContext internals — only consume existing sportStatus, currentSport, and deactivateSport values
The existing handleActivate() logic must remain intact and be called for the inactive path
nonGoals: Adding new fields or methods to SportContext
Changing the visual styling or layout of the activation modal beyond the button label
Modifying Map or Events filtering logic
